segments.text: The wife of one of the sons of the prophets appealed to Elisha,
My husband, your servant, is dead, and you know that he honored the Lord. But now to pay his debts his creditor is coming to take my two sons as his slaves!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What can I do to help you?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: asked Elisha.
Tell me, what do you have in your house?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: I, your servant, don't have anything in my house except a jar of olive oil,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she replied.
Go and borrow empty jars from your neighbors—as many as possible, not just a few,
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Elisha told her.
Then go inside, shut the door behind you and your sons, and start pouring olive oil into all these jars, placing the full jars to one side.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: She left Elisha, went home, and shut the door behind her and her sons. They brought the jars to her and she kept on pouring.
When all the jars were full, she told her son,
Bring me another one.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But he replied,
There aren't any jars left.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the olive oil stopped flowing.
She went and told the man of God what had happened, and he said,
Go and sell the olive oil and pay your debts, and you and your sons can live on what's left.
segments.speaker: She went and

segments.text: One day as Elisha was passing through Shunem, a wealthy woman who lived there convinced him to have a meal. After that, whenever he was passing by he would stop there to eat.
She told her husband,
I'm sure that this man who regularly visits us is a holy man of God.
segments.speaker: She

segments.text: Please let's make a small room on the roof,. We can put a bed, a table, a chair, and a lamp in it for him. Then he can stay there whenever he visits us.
segments.speaker: She

segments.text: One day Elisha arrived and went up to his room and lay down.
He said to his servant Gehazi,
Ask the Shunammite woman to come here.
segments.speaker: He

segments.text: Gehazi called her and she came to see Elisha.
Elisha said to Gehazi,
Please tell her, ‘You have gone to a lot of trouble on our behalf. Now what can we do for you? Do you want us to speak for you to the king or the army commander?’
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: I live with my own people,
segments.speaker: she

segments.text: she replied.
After she had left,  Elisha asked,
What can we do for her?
segments.speaker: she

segments.text: She doesn't have a son, and her husband is old,
segments.speaker: Gehazi

segments.text: Gehazi replied.
Elisha said,
Ask her to come back.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So Gehazi called her, and she came and stood by the door.
Elisha told her,
Around this time next year, you will be holding a son in your arms.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: No, my lord!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she responded.
Man of God, don't tell your servant lies!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But the woman did indeed become pregnant, and the next year around that same time she gave birth to a son, just as Elisha had promised her.
The child grew up, but one day when he went out to see his father who was with the reapers,
he complained to his father,
My head hurts! My head hurts!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His father told one of his servants,
Carry him back to his mother.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The servant picked him up and took him back to his mother. The boy sat on her lap until noon, and then he died.
She went upstairs and laid him on the bed of the man of God. Then she shut the door behind her and left.
She called her husband and said,
Please send me one of the servants and a donkey so I can run to the man of God and come back.
segments.speaker: She

segments.text: Why do you need to go and see him today?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he asked.
It's not the New Moon or the Sabbath.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Don't worry about it,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she replied.
She put the saddle on the donkey and told her servant,
Let's go quickly! Don't slow down for me unless I tell you to!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So she set off, and went to the man of God who was at Mount Carmel. When he saw her way in the distance, the man of God told his servant Gehazi,
Look! There's the Shunammite woman!
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Please run to meet her and ask her, ‘Is everything fine with you, your husband, and your boy?’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Everything's fine,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she replied.
But when she got to the man of God at the mountain, she grabbed hold of his feet. Gehazi came over to push her away, but the man of God said,
Leave her alone, for she's in terrible misery, but the Lord has hidden it from me and has not explained it to me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Did I ask you for a son, my lord?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she asked.
Didn't I tell you, ‘Don't tell me lies’?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Elisha said to Gehazi,
Tie up your cloak in your belt, pick up my staff, and go! Don't even say hello to anyone you meet, and if anyone says hello to you, don't reply. Place my staff on the boy's face.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: But the boy's mother said,
As the Lord lives and as you live, I'm not leaving without you!
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So he got up and went with her.
Gehazi ran on ahead and placed the staff on the boy's face, but there was no sound or sign of life. So Gehazi went back to meet Elisha and told him,
The boy hasn't woken up.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: When Elisha got to the house, there was the boy, lying dead on his bed.
He went in, shut the door behind them both, and prayed to the Lord.
Then he got on the bed and lay on top of the boy, and put his mouth on the boy's mouth, his eyes on the boy's eyes, his hands on the boy's hands. As he stretched out on him, the boy's body warmed up.
Elisha got up, walked back and forth once in the room, and then got back on the bed and stretched out on him again. The boy sneezed seven times and then opened his eyes.
Elisha called Gehazi and said,
Ask the Shunammite woman to come.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So he did. When she arrived, Elisha said to her,
Here's your son. You can pick him up.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: She came in, fell at his feet, and bowed to the ground. Then she picked up her son and left.
When Elisha went back to Gilgal, there was a famine in that area. The sons of the prophets were sitting at his feet, and he said to his servant,
Use the large pot and boil some stew for the sons of the prophets.
segments.speaker: the Son

segments.text: One of them went out into the countryside to pick herbs. He found a wild vine and picked as many wild gourds as his cloak could hold. Then he came back and chopped them up into the pot of stew. But nobody knew they were dangerous to eat.
They served it to the men to eat, but when they tasted the stew they shouted,
There's death in the pot, man of God!
segments.speaker: the Son

segments.text: They couldn't eat it.
Elisha said,
Get some flour.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: He threw it into the pot, and said,
Serve it to the people to eat.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: There was nothing bad to eat in the pot.
A man from Baal-shalishah came to the man of God with a sack of firstfruits—the first grain of the year, along with twenty loaves of barley bread.
Give it to the people to eat,
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: said Elisha.
How can I serve just twenty loaves to a hundred men?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: his servant asked.
Give it to the people to eat,
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: said Elisha,
for this is what the Lord says: ‘They will eat and there will still be some left over.’
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So he served the bread to them. They ate, and had some left over, just as the Lord had said.
